meta.title: AI Token Ledger Explained
meta.subtitle: The emerging system of record for AI work — metering, tracing, storing, and governing every token transaction
meta.author: Imbila.AI

summary: An AI Token Ledger is a composable system of record that meters, logs, attributes, and audits every unit of work performed by AI agents and LLM-powered applications. It treats the token as an atomic unit of economic activity. No single product owns this space — the architecture is assembled from five layers: observability (Langfuse, Helicone, OpenTelemetry), API gateway (LiteLLM, Portkey), event/workflow engine (Temporal, Inngest, Kafka), immutable ledger (PostgreSQL append-only, immudb), and policy engine (OPA).

layers.name: Observability & Tracing
layers.description: Captures token usage, latency, cost, prompts, responses, and traces across agent steps.

tools.name: Langfuse
tools.description: Open-source LLM engineering platform (YC W23). Tracks traces, sessions, token usage, cost per request. Native OTel ingestion since v3. Self-hostable via Docker/Kubernetes. Architecture: Postgres + ClickHouse + Redis + S3.

tools.name: Helicone
tools.description: Open-source LLM observability (YC W23). Proxy-based one-line integration. Rust-based AI gateway for low-latency proxying. Cost, latency, usage, caching, analytics.

tools.name: OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions
tools.description: Emerging industry standard for LLM telemetry. Defines span attributes for gen_ai.usage.input_tokens, gen_ai.usage.output_tokens, gen_ai.request.model. Agent-level conventions for create_agent and invoke_agent. Status: experimental.

tools.name: OpenLLMetry
tools.description: Extends OTel semantic conventions for GenAI. Instrumentation libraries for Python, TypeScript, Go, Ruby. Contributors lead the OTel GenAI SIG working group.

layers.name: API Gateway / Proxy
layers.description: Centralises all LLM calls through a single control point. Normalises requests across providers. Enforces rate limits, budgets, routing. The natural metering point.

tools.name: LiteLLM
tools.description: Python SDK + Proxy Server (AI Gateway). Calls 100+ LLMs in OpenAI-compatible format. Multi-tenant cost tracking. Virtual keys. Budget enforcement per key/team. 8ms P95 latency at 1K RPS. Native Langfuse integration.

tools.name: Portkey AI Gateway
tools.description: <1ms latency, 122KB footprint. Routes to 1,600+ models. Load balancing, fallbacks, retries, guardrails, cost tracking. 10B+ tokens daily. Open-source LLM pricing DB for 2,300+ models.

tools.name: Helicone AI Gateway
tools.description: Rust-based, fully open-source. Smart routing, caching, rate limiting, tracing, fallbacks. 20+ providers.

layers.name: Event & Workflow Engine
layers.description: Treats every agent action as a durable event. Provides replayability, fault tolerance, and audit trails across multi-step workflows.

tools.name: Temporal
tools.description: Durable execution platform. Fork of Uber's Cadence. Workflows survive process failures, network partitions. Complete execution history. SDKs for Go, Java, PHP, TypeScript, Python. Used by Stripe, Netflix, Datadog, Coinbase, Snap.

tools.name: Inngest
tools.description: Serverless event-driven workflow engine. Functions invoked via HTTP, no worker fleet. Built-in retries, scheduling, concurrency control. Self-hostable.

tools.name: Apache Kafka
tools.description: Distributed event streaming platform. Append-only commit log. De facto backbone for high-throughput event pipelines.

layers.name: Immutable Ledger / Storage
layers.description: Stores permanent, tamper-proof record of all token transactions. Most underdeveloped layer — no dominant open-source AI token ledger exists.

tools.name: PostgreSQL (append-only)
tools.description: Most common pragmatic choice. INSERT-only patterns, triggers to prevent UPDATE/DELETE, timestamped versioning. No cryptographic tamper-proofing but operationally sufficient.

tools.name: immudb
tools.description: Open-source immutable database. Cryptographic verification via Merkle trees. SQL and Key-Value access. Millions of TPS. FIPS-compliant. Time-travel queries. Used in financial services, government, defence.

tools.name: ClickHouse
tools.description: Column-oriented OLAP database. Used by Langfuse as analytics backend. Excellent for aggregation queries over token usage data.

layers.name: Policy & Governance
layers.description: Enforces rules about who can spend what, which tools agents can use, when human approval is required. Separates policy logic from application code.

tools.name: Open Policy Agent (OPA)
tools.description: General-purpose policy engine. CNCF Graduated (2021). Policy-as-code using Rego. Used by Netflix, Intuit. Applicable to AI governance: budget caps, tool access lists, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, model access control.

proven_combinations.name: LiteLLM + Langfuse
proven_combinations.description: Most documented integration. LiteLLM routes and meters; Langfuse traces and analyses. Endorsed by both projects. Lemonade runs this in production.

proven_combinations.name: Helicone + n8n
proven_combinations.description: Helicone publishes an official n8n nodes package for workflow integration.

proven_combinations.name: OpenTelemetry + Langfuse
proven_combinations.description: Langfuse v3 natively ingests OTel spans using GenAI semantic conventions. Compliant with v1.37+.

proven_combinations.name: OpenTelemetry + Datadog
proven_combinations.description: Datadog LLM Observability natively supports OTel GenAI SemConv (v1.37+).

use_when: Running autonomous agents consuming real budget unsupervised
Need cost attribution to customers, projects, or business units for chargeback
Regulated industry requiring auditable agent decisions
Scaling past prototype with multi-provider LLM usage (OpenAI + Anthropic + Bedrock)
skip_when: Single LLM integration with predictable, low-volume usage — provider dashboards sufficient
AI spend is immaterial and doesn't warrant governance infrastructure
No autonomous agent decisions — just human-initiated single-turn completions
Early prototyping where five-layer stack overhead would slow you down

use_cases.title: FinOps for AI
use_cases.description: Track and allocate token spend per team, project, customer. Enforce budget caps. Generate chargeback reports. Gateway + observability provide data; ledger makes it auditable.

use_cases.title: Agent Audit Trails
use_cases.description: Log every autonomous agent decision — tools invoked, reasoning chains, data accessed. Critical for regulated industries and enterprise risk management.

use_cases.title: Multi-Agent Cost Attribution
use_cases.description: When Agent A calls Agent B which calls Agent C across different models, attribute full cost chain back to originating request. Requires trace propagation.

use_cases.title: Compliance & Regulatory Reporting
use_cases.description: Demonstrate AI systems operated within policies. Human-in-the-loop controls respected. Data handling complied with governance. Immutable ledger + policy engine.

use_cases.title: AI-Powered Product Billing
use_cases.description: Accurate token metering per customer request for AI feature billing. Gateway captures usage; ledger provides billing record of truth.

use_cases.title: Incident Investigation & Replay
use_cases.description: Replay exact sequence of calls, prompts, tool invocations. Temporal/Inngest provide replay; Langfuse provides trace detail.

timeline.title: Foundations
timeline.detail: OpenTelemetry formed. OPA accepted into CNCF (2018). Temporal forked from Uber's Cadence.

timeline.title: LLM Era Begins
timeline.detail: GPT-3 launches (2020). Ad-hoc LLM logging. immudb gains financial services and government adoption.

timeline.title: Observability Explodes
timeline.detail: Langfuse and Helicone launch (YC W23). LiteLLM and Portkey gain traction. Gateway + observability + ledger pattern emerges.

timeline.title: Standards Emerge
timeline.detail: OTel GenAI SemConv published (experimental). Agent-level conventions added. AWS QLDB discontinued; immudb becomes primary alternative.

timeline.title: Convergence (Current)
timeline.detail: Langfuse v3 adds native OTel ingestion. LiteLLM processes 10B+ tokens/day. Portkey handles 400B+ tokens for 200+ enterprises. Five-layer architecture solidifies. No unified standard yet.

ledger_schema.description: Minimum viable record for an AI Token Ledger

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this token transaction

fields.name: agent_id
fields.description: Which agent (or agent chain) made the call

fields.name: human_id
fields.description: Which human initiated or is responsible

fields.name: workflow_id
fields.description: Which workflow / session this belongs to

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: OpenTelemetry trace ID for full context
